summary: This is a brief announcements post from Joel Spolsky covering several short updates. He announces two upcoming 'Joel on Software' community dinners in Montréal and Berkeley, following a successful event in Oslo. He mentions a speaking engagement at a Canadian undergraduate software engineering conference. The post also teases an imminent release of FogBUGZ for Unix and Macintosh platforms, with a characteristically hedged timeline.
key_points: Joel organized community dinners where software developers could meet in person, building on a successful Oslo event
FogBUGZ was expanding beyond Windows to Unix and Macintosh platforms, broadening Fog Creek's market
Joel was actively engaging with the next generation of developers through university conference speaking
money_quotes: Basically we'll take over a room in a restaurant, eat, drink, be merry, and talk about software development.
… will ship, I think, on Monday, subject to reality sticking its big ugly face in the way.
key_insight: Building a developer community through in-person gatherings and cross-platform product expansion were key elements of Fog Creek's growth strategy.
